title: as a lovin malta journalist have a look at the information below and write an article out of it:

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
as a lovin malta journalist have a look at the information below and write an article out of it:
Hey dude - we need an article announcing the launch of the booking
functionality on Lovin Restaurants. It is a new collab with a company
called Bookia - so we need to announce the functionality as well as
hype up Bookia a bit.

So far there are 25 restaurants on the Lovin Restaurants website with
this functionality

Here is a quote from them:

Bookia is Malta’s largest homegrown online restaurant booking
platform. Built to help diners find, discover and make reservations
and working with over 300 restaurants across Malta and Gozo. Through
the Bookia online widget / app, diners can book a table and receive
confirmation, in just a few clicks, without the need to call or wait
on hold.


On-the-day automatic reminders of the reservation are automatically
sent, including a Google maps link to easily find the restaurant.
Cancellation is also possible free of charge at the click of a button.
After dining out, Bookia sends a link encouraging diners to leave a
review. Restaurant reviews are created daily, only by people who have
dined out at the restaurant, this way you get real information about
what's hot and what's not.


------------------------------------------------------


"We're thrilled to announce our partnership with Lovin Malta. As
Malta’s most popular restaurant booking platform, Bookia.mt will now
be providing Lovin Malta readers with a quick and easy reservation
experience. Equally, Lovin Malta's extensive reach and expertise in
the local market, will help us showcase the unique dining experiences
available on our platform, to a larger audience. Through this
partnership we will be able to connect with more diners and continue
to grow our business.


We're excited about the opportunities this collaboration presents and
look forward to working together to provide everyone in Malta with a
superior dining experience."


Jonathan Azzopardi Frantz, CEO

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
New Booking Functionality Launched on Lovin Restaurants in Collaboration with Bookia

Lovin Restaurants is delighted to announce the launch of a new booking functionality on our website, made possible through a collaboration with Malta's largest homegrown online restaurant booking platform, Bookia. The startup already works with over 300 restaurants across Malta and Gozo, making it easier than ever for diners to find, discover and make reservations.

We are proud to say that so far, 25 restaurants on the Lovin Restaurants website feature this new functionality. Diners can use the Bookia widget or app to book a table and receive instant confirmation, without the need for tedious phone calls or waiting on hold. Additionally, on-the-day automatic reminders, including a Google Maps link to the restaurant's location, make the dining experience as hassle-free as possible. Should plans change, cancellation is also available free of charge at the click of a button.

Bookia is committed to ensuring that users receive real, helpful information about their dining options, with post-visit review requests sent to each diner. These reviews, created only by those who have dined at the restaurant, provide a trustworthy resource for what's hot and what's not in Malta's dining scene.

Jonathan Azzopardi Frantz, CEO of Bookia, shared his excitement about the collaboration: "We're thrilled to announce our partnership with Lovin Malta. As Malta's most popular restaurant booking platform, Bookia.mt will now be providing Lovin Malta readers with a quick and easy reservation experience. Equally, Lovin Malta's extensive reach and expertise in the local market will help us showcase the unique dining experiences available on our platform, to a larger audience."

Through this partnership, Bookia looks forward to connecting with more diners and growing its business, while Lovin Malta will continue to provide our readers with an exceptional dining experience. Check out our website to explore the restaurants now offering this convenient new booking functionality!

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
GSSE Team Malta Accomodation, Opening Ceremony Details Announced 
 
With less than a month to go for the start of the XIX Games of the Small States of Europe, 
the Maltese Olympic Committee hosted a press briefing presenting the latest updates in the 
run up to the Games.  
 
The Games of the Small States of Europe are one of the largest events to be hosted in Malta 
in the last twenty years. Preparation, with a strong attention to detail,  for this event has taken 
several years, and Malta will be prepared to deliver the best possible result from a technical 
and organisational perspective.   
 
It was announced, that Team Malta will be not be staying with the rest of the international 
delegations but will be staying at the Urban Valley Resort and Spa. The decision to keep 
Maltese athletes separate was to ensure that they have the right environment to focus and 
concentrate on their competitions.  This was announced by Shawn Muscat, General Manager 
of the Resort.  
 
Furthermore it was announced that the national TV broadcaster – Public Broadcasting 
Services will be co-ordinating with other TV Stations and Media houses to give the best and 
widest possible coverage to the public.  This will entail bringing all available technical 
resources together to share footage, content and stories.  The Games are also expecting a 
strong influx of international journalists which will give the Games and Malta substantial 
international exposure. To this effect two media centres will be setup in the National Pool 
Complex and in the newly built Gymnastics centre in Marsa.  Furthermore all venues will 
have media rooms which will enable accredited media to have comfortable workspace.  
 
“We have always had a good relationship with the media and they can rest assured of our co-
operation. We are an open book.  The only thing we ask of journalists is to allow athletes to 
focus on achieving the desired results” remarked Mark Cutajar, Chairperson, Local 
Organising Committee”  
 
It was also announced that the Opening Ceremony will take place on Monday 29th May at 
2045hrs at the Granaries in Floriana.  The event will be of a high level that will be broadcast 
live in all GSSE countries and will showcase the beauty and hospitable nature Malta is 
renowned for, and where athletes will be the centre of attention. 
 
MOC President Julian Pace Bonello expressed this gratitude towards members of the media 
for supporting the Games. Important for various reasons especially our country where we will 
have the opportunity to welcome a substantial number of international guests. It will be a 
great opportunity for them to experience Maltese hospitality and where communities will be 
brought together to support Team Malta to encourage them to the best they can be. 
 
Minister Clifton Grima spoke about the enthusiasm that exists not only amongst athletes, but 
also within the Organizing Committee who have been working on these Games ever since 
Malta was announced as the 2023 Games host.  
 
"The response from my international colleagues – Ministers responsible for Sport from the 
small states of Europe, has been excellent. They are looking forward to be amongst us, and 
the enthusiasm is significant especially since during the pandemic this event did not take 
place. Our country will meet all expectations, because the work that has been done and which 
is currently at its culmination will give the expected results", said Minister Grima. 
 
The XIX Games of the Small States of Europe are being supported by SportMalta, NDSF, 
Welbee’s Supermarket, National Lottery, MITA, General Soft Drinks, Visit Malta, European 
Olympic Committees, Go, Enemed, BOV, Transport Malta, Malta International Airport and 
The Concept Stadium.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
GSSE Team Malta Accommodation and Opening Ceremony Details Revealed

The Maltese Olympic Committee has shared exciting updates in the lead-up to the XIX Games of the Small States of Europe, which begins in less than a month. The event marks one of Malta's largest in the past two decades and has been meticulously planned to ensure exceptional technical and organizational results.

It was announced that Team Malta will not be staying with other international delegations. Instead, they will reside at the Urban Valley Resort and Spa to maintain a focused environment for optimal performance in their competitions.

National TV broadcaster Public Broadcasting Services will be collaborating with other TV stations and media houses to deliver the most extensive coverage possible of the games. The event is expecting a substantial number of international journalists, providing both Malta and the Games with significant global exposure. Two media centers are being set up at the National Pool Complex and the newly built Gymnastics center in Marsa.

The Opening Ceremony for the Games will take place on Monday, 29th May at 20:45 hrs at the Granaries in Floriana, showcasing the beauty and hospitality Malta is known for, with athletes in the spotlight.

MOC President Julian Pace Bonello expressed gratitude to the media for supporting the Games and highlighted the significance of this event for Malta, which provides an opportunity for international guests to experience Maltese hospitality and unite communities. Minister Clifton Grima also spoke about the enthusiasm among athletes and the Organizing Committee in anticipation of the games.

The XIX Games of the Small States of Europe are being supported by various organizations, such as SportMalta, NDSF, Welbee's Supermarket, National Lottery, MITA, General Soft Drinks, Visit Malta, European Olympic Committees, Go, Enemed, BOV, Transport Malta, Malta International Airport, and The Concept Stadium.
